subject: [PATCH v2] nfsd: fix file change detection in CB_GETATTR

reviews.author: Scott Mayhew
reviews.summary: reviewer questioned why the existing change attribute doesn't already account for pending buffered data, pointing out that dirty pages/folios are tracked on the nfs_inode and an elevated change attribute is sent in CB_GETATTR replies
reviews.raw_body:
I guess I don't understand why the client's usage of the change
attribute doesn't already cover that.

If an application calls write() but not fsync(), close(), etc, and the
background flushers haven't kicked in yet, then the dirty pages/folios
will be tracked on the nfs_inode and the client will send the elevated
change attribute in the CB_GETATTR reply.

What am I missing?

2026-04-06.patch_summary: This patch fixes an issue where file change detection was not working correctly in the NFS daemon's (nfsd) CB_GETATTR operation, which is used to retrieve attributes of a file. The problem was likely due to incorrect handling of buffered data on the client side. To fix this, the patch may involve adding a new attribute or modifying existing behavior to ensure accurate change detection.
